blocks.text: DTC P2123: Gas pedal Pedal Position Sensor 1 voltage high

The accelerator pedal position sensor is a component of the DBW system that converts the accelerator pedal position into an electrical signal, and the PGM-FI ECU controls the throttle actuator to synchronize the accelerator pedal position with the throttle valve position based on the signal from the accelerator pedal position sensor. The PGM-FI ECU controls the throttle actuator to synchronize the throttle pedal position and throttle valve position. If the accelerator pedal position sensor 1 output voltage remains above a predetermined voltage for a predetermined period of time, the PGM-FI ECU judges a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows: DTC requesting the prohibition of detection of the relevant DTC

blocks.text: Accelerator Pedal Position Sensor 1 output voltage [AP Sensor 1] remains 4.9V or higher for 0.2 seconds or longer.

items.text: Short-circuit in power supply between PGM-FI ECU and accelerator pedal position sensor 1 cord (APS1 line)
Disconnection of cord (SG line) between PGM-FI ECU and accelerator pedal position sensor 1

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
